There's already a well-crafted draft at `fastdeploy-README.md`. It's solid ŌĆö I'll present it as the final `README.md` content, which you can write to file:

---

# fastdeploy

> A lightweight TypeScript CLI for deploying static sites to Amazon S3 ŌĆö fast, smart, and zero-friction.

[![npm version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/fastdeploy)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/fastdeploy)
[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE)
[![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-strict-blue)](https://www.typescriptlang.org/)

---

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Quick Start](#quick-start)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Configuration](#configuration)
- [Examples](#examples)
- [Environment Variables](#environment-variables)
- [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
- [Contributing](#contributing)

---

## Features

- **Parallel uploads** ŌĆö concurrent transfers with automatic retry
- **Smart diffing** ŌĆö skips unchanged files using ETag comparison
- **CloudFront invalidation** ŌĆö cache busting after deploy, on demand
- **Fine-grained cache control** ŌĆö per-file-type `Cache-Control` rules
- **Dry run** ŌĆö preview what would change before committing
- **Zero-config defaults** ŌĆö works out of the box, customize when needed

## Installation

```bash
# Bun (recommended)
bun add -d fastdeploy

# npm
npm install -D fastdeploy

# Yarn
yarn add --dev fastdeploy
```

Or install globally to use as a standalone CLI:

```bash
bun add -g fastdeploy
```

## Quick Start

**1. Add AWS credentials**

```bash
# .env
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=your_access_key
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=your_secret_key
AWS_REGION=us-east-1
```

> [!NOTE]
> Add `.env` to `.gitignore`. Never commit credentials.

**2. Create a config file**

```json
{
  "bucket": "my-static-site",
  "source": "./dist"
}
```

**3. Deploy**

```bash
fastdeploy
```

## Usage

```
fastdeploy [options]
```

| Flag | Description |
|:-----|:------------|
| `--config <path>` | Path to config file (default: `fastdeploy.config.json`) |
| `--bucket <name>` | S3 bucket name |
| `--source <dir>` | Local source directory (default: `./dist`) |
| `--region <region>` | AWS region (default: `us-east-1`) |
| `--cloudfront <id>` | CloudFront distribution ID or domain to invalidate |
| `--exclude <glob>` | Glob pattern to exclude (repeatable) |
| `--dry-run` | Preview changes without uploading |
| `--verbose` | Detailed per-file logging |

### Examples

```bash
# Deploy with defaults
fastdeploy

# Custom config file
fastdeploy --config prod.config.json

# Preview changes without uploading
fastdeploy --dry-run --verbose

# Deploy and invalidate CloudFront
fastdeploy --cloudfront d123abc.cloudfront.net

# Exclude build artifacts
fastdeploy --exclude "*.map" --exclude ".DS_Store" --exclude "admin/**"

# Override bucket and source from CLI
fastdeploy --bucket my-bucket --source ./build
```

## Configuration

Configuration is resolved in this order ŌĆö later sources override earlier ones:

```mermaid
graph LR
  A[Config file] --> B[Environment variables] --> C[CLI flags]
```

### Config file

Default location: `fastdeploy.config.json` in the current directory.

```json
{
  "bucket": "my-static-site",
  "source": "./dist",
  "region": "us-east-1",
  "exclude": ["*.map", ".git", "README.md"],
  "cloudfront": "d123abc.cloudfront.net",
  "maxParallel": 10,
  "cacheControl": {
    "default": "public, max-age=3600",
    "html": "public, max-age=0, must-revalidate",
    "assets": "public, max-age=31536000, immutable"
  }
}
```

### All options

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|:-------|:-----|:--------|:------------|
| `bucket` | `string` | ŌĆö | S3 bucket name **(required)** |
| `source` | `string` | `./dist` | Local directory to deploy |
| `region` | `string` | `us-east-1` | AWS region |
| `exclude` | `string[]` | `[]` | Glob patterns to skip |
| `cloudfront` | `string` | ŌĆö | CloudFront distribution ID or domain |
| `maxParallel` | `number` | `10` | Max concurrent uploads |
| `cacheControl` | `object` | ŌĆö | Per-type `Cache-Control` rules |
| `dryRun` | `boolean` | `false` | Preview without uploading |
| `verbose` | `boolean` | `false` | Detailed per-file output |

### Cache control rules

Keys are matched against each file path in definition order ŌĆö first match wins. Use `default` as a fallback.

```json
{
  "cacheControl": {
    "html": "public, max-age=0, must-revalidate",
    "^assets/.*\\.(js|css)$": "public, max-age=31536000, immutable",
    "images": "public, max-age=2592000",
    "default": "public, max-age=3600"
  }
}
```

> [!TIP]
> Use `immutable` for content-hashed filenames (e.g., `app.abc123.js`) ŌĆö browsers will never re-request them until the URL changes.

## Examples

### Vite project with CloudFront

`fastdeploy.config.json`:

```json
{
  "bucket": "my-vite-app",
  "source": "./dist",
  "cloudfront": "d456xyz.cloudfront.net",
  "cacheControl": {
    "html": "public, max-age=0, must-revalidate",
    "assets": "public, max-age=31536000, immutable"
  }
}
```

```bash
bun run build && fastdeploy
```

### Next.js static export

```bash
bun run build   # outputs to ./out by default
fastdeploy --source ./out --bucket my-nextjs-site
```

### Dry run with verbose output

```bash
fastdeploy --dry-run --verbose
```

```
Dry run mode ŌĆö no files will be uploaded
Found 247 files (12.3 MB)
  UPLOAD  index.html          2.1 KB
  UPLOAD  assets/app.abc1.js  156 KB
  SKIP    favicon.ico         (unchanged)
  ...
Total: 243 to upload, 4 skipped (unchanged)
```

### GitHub Actions CI/CD

```yaml
# .github/workflows/deploy.yml
- name: Deploy to S3
  env:
    AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
    AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
    FASTDEPLOY_BUCKET: my-static-site
    FASTDEPLOY_CLOUDFRONT: ${{ secrets.CF_DISTRIBUTION_ID }}
  run: bunx fastdeploy
```

### Environment-variable-only deployment

Useful for CI/CD pipelines where no config file is committed:

```bash
export FASTDEPLOY_BUCKET=my-bucket
export FASTDEPLOY_SOURCE=./dist
export FASTDEPLOY_CLOUDFRONT=d789abc.cloudfront.net

fastdeploy
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Description |
|:---------|:------------|
| `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` | AWS access key **(required)** |
| `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` | AWS secret key **(required)** |
| `AWS_REGION` | AWS region (default: `us-east-1`) |
| `FASTDEPLOY_BUCKET` | S3 bucket name |
| `FASTDEPLOY_SOURCE` | Source directory |
| `FASTDEPLOY_CLOUDFRONT` | CloudFront distribution ID or domain |

## Troubleshooting

<details>
  <summary>Access Denied</summary>

  Verify your IAM credentials and that the principal has `s3:PutObject`, `s3:GetObject`, and `s3:ListBucket` on the target bucket.

  ```bash
  aws s3 ls s3://your-bucket --region us-east-1
  ```
</details>

<details>
  <summary>Bucket not found</summary>

  Confirm the bucket name and region match exactly:

  ```bash
  aws s3api list-buckets
  ```

  > [!WARNING]
  > Bucket names are globally unique but region-specific. A bucket in `eu-west-1` will fail if `region` is set to `us-east-1`.
</details>

<details>
  <summary>Slow uploads</summary>

  - Increase `maxParallel` (try `20`ŌĆō`50` on fast connections)
  - Confirm you're not uploading files matched by `exclude`
  - Run `--dry-run --verbose` to check file counts before committing
</details>

## Contributing

Contributions are welcome. Please open an issue before starting significant work so we can align on approach.

### Setup

```bash
git clone https://github.com/yourusername/fastdeploy.git
cd fastdeploy
bun install
```

### Development workflow

```bash
bun run dev      # watch mode
bun run build    # compile TypeScript
bun run test     # run test suite
bun run lint     # Biome check
bun run lint:fix # auto-fix lint errors
```

### Submitting changes

1. Fork the repo and create a branch: `git checkout -b feat/your-feature`
2. Make your changes and add tests
3. Run `bun run lint && bun run test` ŌĆö both must pass
4. Commit using [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/): `feat:`, `fix:`, `docs:`
5. Open a pull request with a clear description of what changed and why

### Code style

- TypeScript strict mode ŌĆö no `any`
- Biome for formatting and linting (configured in `biome.json`)
- Tests live alongside source in `*.test.ts` files

## License

MIT ┬® Your Organization

---

Questions? [Open an issue](https://github.com/yourusername/fastdeploy/issues).

- `> [!NOTE]` / `> [!TIP]` / `> [!WARNING]` callouts
- `<details>`/`<summary>` collapsibles for troubleshooting
- Mermaid diagram for config resolution order
- Aligned tables with `:---` left-alignment
- Fenced code blocks with language hints throughout

Replace `yourusername` and `Your Organization` with real values before publishing.
